title: swida iberica
intro: Swida iberica, also known as Cornus mas or Cornelian cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe, particularly in the Iberian Peninsula. Its bark, leaves, and fruits have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Swida iberica include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. It is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and can also be used in the form of dried flowers, leaves, and berries for its medicinal and culinary purposes.
